meta.plugin: Aiven tiered-storage-for-apache-kafka v1.1.1 (S3 backend; plugin compression+encryption OFF)
meta.stored_bytes: tiered S3 bytes per topic prefix (rolled segments only; active local segment excluded — same for every config). Includes S4 sidecars for the s4 endpoint.
meta.not_exercised: multi-broker replication; plugin-level compression. The --logical-etag negative is captured in logical_etag_negative.txt (not required for Kafka, either checksum mode).
meta.remote_fetch_ms: single cold consume per config (broker restarted -> empty chunk cache; rolled segments local-deleted so served from S3). consumer-perf-test fetch.time.ms. Single-sample and noisy; the transferable signal is only that there is no consistent S4 penalty, not the exact ms.

fairness_check.note: roll-point-independent check: one tiered `none` segment GET back through S4 (logical) vs its stored compressed size — confirms the none-row saving is the per-segment zstd ratio, not a counting artifact.

local_log_counts_after_tiering.note: local .log files per topic after tiering+local-retention (before the cold-fetch restart). 1 = only the active segment remains local, so consume-from-earliest reads rolled segments from S3.
